about freedom and they circle the number that fits with their opinions and they do that for a number of questions and then they give the clipboard with their sheet back to the experimenter who uses a sleight of hand to actually to copy to kind of glue onto the sheet a question that spells the opposite of the original question so if the original question was you know I think that freedom matters more than than safety then the new question reads I think that safety matters more than freedom

But I do think that we're not that gullible and that if you gave people a really, a motive that had nothing to do with your actual motives, most people would call you up on it and indeed most people do and most of the justifications we give are actually decent justifications.
The situation in which it's more likely to have justifications that are really made up is when they suit everybody. So usually when you have to justify something is because you're, you know, you expect that someone else, either you can see someone else is judging you, they're asking you where did you do this? But most of the time we anticipate other people judging us and we give them reasons for what we've done to anticipate being judged negatively so if you.

But sometimes as a group of people or even as a society.
We're happy to have justifications that we could present to someone else, but they never really get properly evaluated. And these are the ones for which it's easier to get away with stuff that is less plausible.
To give you examples like in most or maybe every small scale society, people believe in witches or in some form of supernatural entity that causes many or kind of most misfortunes.
And it's possible that people have good reasons for wanting to be able to blame a given individual for a given misfortune. And it's not just one person. It's like the group as a whole is happy enough to converge on the idea that, well, you know, we don't have to all share the responsibility for that guy's misfortune. We're just going to say it's that guy's fault. And except for that guy, everybody else is kind of happy to believe that. And so they can make up justifications that from an outside perspective might not be super convincing.
But given that everybody is kind of looking for a justification, the threshold for or the criteria are kind of quite low.
